title: Ensure security group changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure security group changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. Security groups are stateful packet filters that control ingress and egress traffic within a VPC. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established to detect changes to security groups.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to security groups will help ensure that resources and services are not unintentionally exposed.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: Internal Only - General o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = CreateSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = DeleteSecurityGroup) }" 4. Note the <security-group-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <security-group-changes-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query "MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<security-group-changes-metric>]" 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: Internal Only - General 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for security groups changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <security-group-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<security-group-changes-metric>,metricNamespace="CISBenchmark",metricValue=1 --filter-pattern "{ ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = CreateSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = DeleteSecurityGroup) }" Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <security-group-changes-alarm> --metric-name <security-group-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace "CISBenchmark" --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: This may require additional 'tuning' to eliminate false positives and filter out expected activity so that anomalies are easier to detect.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_security_group_changes_monitoring_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls / Boto3 SDK Methods to Use:**
   - Use the `boto3` methods:
     - `cloudtrail.describe_trails()` to list all CloudTrail trails.
     - `cloudtrail.get_trail_status(Name=<trail_name>)` to check if the trail is logging.
     - `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail_name>)` to verify event selectors.
     - `logs.describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail_log_group_name>)` to check for the specific metric filter.
     - `cloudwatch.describe_alarms()` to verify that relevant CloudWatch alarms exist.
     - `sns.list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns_topic_arn>)` to check for subscribers on the SNS topic.

2. **AWS CLI Commands (if applicable):**
   - Equivalent AWS CLI commands can be used for operations:
     - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
     - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
     - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
     - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
     - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms`
     - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - All CloudTrail trails across the AWS account.
   - The log groups associated with each multi-region CloudTrail trail.
   - The metric filters in place for each log group.
   - CloudWatch metrics and alarms related to the specific filter.
   - The SNS topics associated with the alerts.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail.
   - The active trail must have logging enabled.
   - The trail must have event selectors configured to capture all management events.
   - The metric filter should have the prescribed filter pattern for security group changes.
   - There must be an active CloudWatch alarm that uses the relevant metric.
   - The SNS topic associated with the alarm must have at least one active subscriber.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - Pass if at least one active multi-region trail exists with logging enabled and capturing management events.
   - Pass if the log group has a metric filter with the specified pattern for security group changes.
   - Pass if there is an associated CloudWatch alarm that triggers for those metric changes.
   - Pass if the SNS topic attached to the alarm has at least one active subscription.

6. **Example:**
   - Use `boto3` calls to confirm:
     - Call `cloudtrail.describe_trails()` and check for a trail that has `IsMultiRegionTrail=True`.
     - Call `cloudtrail.get_trail_status(Name=<active_trail_name>)` and ensure `IsLogging=True`.
     - Call `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors(TrailName=<active_trail_name>)` and verify `IncludeManagementEvents=True`.
     - Use `logs.describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<log_group_name>)` to confirm the presence of the metric filter for security group changes.
     - List CloudWatch alarms and ensure at least one is configured with the proper metric.
     - Check the SNS subscriptions to confirm that they have valid endpoints.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE  
- The classification as Automated is correct. The audit can be effectively conducted using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI, which are well-suited for checking configurations and statuses of AWS services like CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8  
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The boto3 methods and CLI commands listed are valid for the tasks described. However, there are some areas that could be enhanced for completeness and clarity:
  - The `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors()` should explicitly check for the presence of management events and possibly data events if required.
  - The program name `aws_cloudtrail_security_group_changes_monitoring_enabled` is somewhat descriptive but could be more specific to include the aspect of "real-time monitoring" or "alerting".

**IMPROVEMENTS:**  
- **Event Selector Details:** Ensure that the `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors()` call checks for both management and data events if applicable, as security group changes might be captured under different event types.
- **Metric Filter Pattern:** The specific pattern for detecting security group changes should be detailed. This typically involves filtering for specific API calls like `AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress`, `RevokeSecurityGroupIngress`, etc.
- **Program Name:** Consider renaming to `aws_cloudtrail_security_group_change_alerts_configured` to better reflect the focus on alerting and configuration.
- **Additional Checks:** Verify the retention period of CloudTrail logs to ensure compliance with data retention policies.
- **SNS Subscription Validation:** Ensure that SNS subscriptions are not only active but also configured to notify the appropriate stakeholders.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH  
- The assessment is well-grounded in AWS capabilities, and the approach is technically sound, with minor areas for improvement.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify  
- Enhance the event selector check to ensure comprehensive coverage of relevant events.
- Clarify the metric filter pattern for detecting security group changes.
- Consider a more descriptive program name to reflect the alerting aspect.
- Verify the retention settings of CloudTrail logs as part of the audit.
